preset_reason: paper-candidate exact V20 portfolio: native HGA-TGBC UB, full frontier relaxation certificate, optional exact interval oracle merge
certificate: gamma-frontier certificate: every relevant Gini interval was either closed with exact pricing or fathomed by a valid interval lower bound above the verified incumbent objective
incumbent_selection_reason: minimum verifier-passed route plan from seeded paper primal heuristic
incumbent_provenance: same-run native HGA-TGBC paper primal heuristic, verifier-gated UB only
notes: RunConfigSnapshot: algorithm_preset=paper-exact-v20-certificate, preset_certificate_scope=exact_v20_frontier_relaxation_or_interval_oracle_certificate, pricing_engine=exact-label, final_pricing_engine=exact-label, column_tracks=elementary-only, rmp_column_space=elementary, relaxed_columns_in_rmp=false, large_instance_mode=off, station_set_backend=uint64, route_mask_all_subset_enumeration_enabled=true, incumbent_archive_auto=false, compact_fallback_enabled=false
parsed Hybrid GA text format; distances rebuilt from points at speed factor 1.5

Gamma-frontier diagnostic covers Gini intervals with fixed-Gini interval branch-price trees. It reports optimal only if every relevant interval is closed or bound-fathomed and the aggregated lower bound reaches the incumbent. Each interval also uses the valid trivial bound objective>=G>=interval_floor and a final-inventory pickup/route/Gini relaxation bound when it solves.
